sections.paragraph:
pr. cessation, hence-

(A) subst. m.-

(1) end, extremity. אַפְסֵי אֶרֶץ “end s of the earth,” poet. and hyperbol. used of the extreme limits of the earth, Psa 2:8 22:28 , and elsewhere.

(2) Dual אַפְסַיִם of the extremities, i.e. the soles of the feet. Thus Eze 47:3, מֵי אַפְסַיִם “water of the soles,” i.e. not deep, which would only wet the soles of the feet. Comp. פַּס. Ch., Syr., Vulg. render it ancles.

(B) adv.-

(1) no farther, i.e. לֹא עֹוד, Isa 5:8 Amo 6:10 Deu 32:36. Also moreover not, Isa 45:6 46:9 . There is once added עֹוד, 2Sa 9:3 also with Yod paragogic, Isa 47:8, 10 Isa 47:10 Zep 2:15, אֲנִי וְאַפְסִי עֹוד “I am, and there is none besides.”

(2) not, Isa 54:15 בְּאֶפֶס like בְּלֹא without, Job 7:6 Dan 8:25 Pro 14:28.

(3) nothing, Isa 41:12, 29 Isa 41:29מֵאֶפֶס id. prop.of nothing, Isa 40:17 בְּאֶפֶס “on account of nothing,” i.e. without cause, Isa 52:4.

(4) adv. of restraining; only, Num 22:35 (comp. verse 20 Num 22:20); 23:13 .

(5) Conj. אֶפֶס כִּי pr. only that, simply, for the conjunction however, nevertheless, Num 13:28 Deu 15:4 Amo 9:8.
